title: Printing a Quote from Winston Churchill

content.problemStatement:
In this task, you need to identify and correct errors in the provided Python code so that it outputs the following message in the proper format:

`"Success is not final,
Failure is not fatal: It is the courage to continue that counts."
    - Winston Churchill
`
content.inputFormat: No input required. If the first token of the input is an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the quote must be printed T times; otherwise it is printed once.
content.outputFormat:
Print exactly the following text (including the double quotes and line breaks):
"Success is not final,
Failure is not fatal: It is the courage to continue that counts."
    - Winston Churchill

sampleTestCases.output: \"Success is not final,\nFailure is not fatal: It is the courage to continue that counts.\"\n    - Winston Churchill
sampleTestCases.explanation: No input is provided, so the program prints the quote exactly once.

sampleTestCases.output:

    - Winston Churchill
"Success is not final,

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first token is 3, therefore the quote is printed three times, each block separated by a newline.

solution.approach: Read the whole input. Trim leading whitespace and try to parse the first token as an integer. If parsing succeeds and the value is within the allowed range, use it as T; otherwise default T to 1. Define the required quote as a constant string (including the surrounding double quotes and the newline before the author). Output the quote T times, separating consecutive copies with a single newline character. The algorithm runs in O(T) time and O(1) additional space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time output per repetition of the quote.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few scalar variables and the constant quote string are stored.
